displayTitle: The Filter That Throws Away a Real Flaw

beats.narration: Frequency, drive, and gain are set. Two lines remain — filters and thresholds — and they decide what the instrument keeps and what it calls.

payload.title: FILTERING & THRESHOLDS
payload.sub: Clean the signal, then automate the accept-reject call.

beats.narration: First, the trap. Filters never touch the test frequency. They act on the signal frequency motion creates — roughly scan speed over the probe's response width.
High-pass strips slow drift below the flaw band; low-pass strips fast grass above it. The band-pass works only because the flaw rides inside.

payload.heading: One scan, the band-pass at work
payload.note: HIGH-PASS BELOW · FLAW BAND · LOW-PASS ABOVE

beats.narration: So filters belong to a scan speed. Slow down and the flaw drops below the high-pass — gradual wall thinning vanishes like drift.

chips.s: Flaw falls below the high-pass, masked
Pulse rounds off below the low-pass
Run the reference at production speed

beats.narration: The threshold draws the alarm line. Prove it dynamically — the reference notch must trip every pass. Never raise it to quiet a noisy line.

payload.sub: A flaw under the line is an escape no one re-makes.
